vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

requirements: Carry nacelle loads into fuselage spine
Transfer wing spar motion into fuselage
Heat dissipation from avionics bay
Data and power cabling for avionics
Service access for maintenance and inspection

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Tilt axis with bearing seats, servo linkage, rotation clearance arc

physics_paths.description: Rotation of the tilt servo is transferred through the tilt axis to the bearing seat.
The tilt linkage connects the bearing seat to the nacelle, allowing controlled tilting.
Heat generated by the tilt servo is dissipated through the heat sink to the ambient environment.
Electrical power is supplied from the battery pack to the tilt servo via the harness.
The maintenance team accesses the tilt servo for servicing through the access cover.

verification_focus.description: Ensure the tilt axis and bearing seat provide sufficient clearance for rotation without binding.
Verify that the heat sink design effectively dissipates heat from the tilt servo.
Confirm that the harness provides reliable power to the tilt servo.
Ensure the access cover allows easy access for maintenance and replacement of the tilt servo.

risk.desc: Potential thermal dissipation issues may arise if the battery bay does not effectively vent heat to the fuselage.
risk.mit: Implement additional vent channels and ensure thermal zones are correctly placed.

risk.desc: If the BMS connector is not properly aligned, it could lead to electrical issues.
risk.mit: Ensure alignment fixtures are precise and conduct regular checks during assembly.

risk.desc: Service access panel may be difficult to remove if not designed with ease of access in mind.
risk.mit: Design the service access panel with quick-release features and ensure it is easily removable.

risk.desc: Fastener patterns may not provide adequate preload if not correctly torqued.
risk.mit: Train assembly personnel on proper torque application and conduct regular torque checks.

risk.desc: Incorrect routing of harnesses may cause strain relief issues and potential failure points.
risk.mit: Ensure harnesses are routed through protected channels and strain relief features are implemented.
